Selected Student Evaluations
“Professor was great for it being his first time teaching ever. I really enjoyed his lectures and the helpfulness he provided to us students! 1000000% recommend.”
“He lays the material out very well and teaches it in a way that translates well to the tests”
“Fantastic class with an amazing instructor. Very clear and concise”
“Very good, i wish there were more teachers like this one. He kept us pretty on course and made the course digestible and tried his best to give us a great semester.”
“JP was awesome. He pointed out being new to teaching, but the concepts in this class were made so simple and easy because of him. Ive ended the semester with the necessary knowledge and had almost no struggle throughout because of JP.”
“The class can be a little slow at times, but he did his best to make it engaging by connecting the learning to real life situations, which was very helpful. This was his first year teaching and, for future students sake, I hope he keeps teaching”

title: Policy Work
text:
I have worked as a policy analyst in the Executive and Legislative branches of City of Porto Alegre, Brazil, where I got to work on important economic reforms. Most notably, a major business licensing reform that reduced the average time to open a business from 245 to 18 days.
I am also a fellow at Instituto Millenium, one of Brazil’s leading policy think tanks, and I discuss policy issues as a regular contributor to Exame.

Policy Reforms





Title


Law


Effect


Links






Reduction of Bureaucracy (2)


Law nº 12,713/2020


Establishes tax-payer number as a single document in public services







Municipal Bill of Economic Freedom


Supplemental Law nº 876/2020


Exempts low risk business from licensing







End of Monopoly for City Tour Bus Routes


Law nº 12,713/2020


Private companies can operate freely, deciding their own routes







Reduction of Bureaucracy (1)


Law nº 12,411/2018


Exempts citizens from providing notarized copies and proof of address in public services

title: JP Bastos | Welcome
text:
Download my CV

I am a PhD candidate at the Department of Agricultural & Applied Economics, and a Research Assistant at the Free Market Institute, both at Texas Tech University.
My research focuses on the political economy of development. I am particularly interested in answering empirical questions about the role of economic and political institutions for development within the Latin American context, using modern causal inference techniques.
My Job Market Paper investigates the private returns to political office for Brazilian mayors, using a regression discontinuity design around close election races. It was awarded the 2026 Elinor and Vincent Ostrom Prize for best paper by a graduate student at the Annual Meetings of the Public Choice Society.
I have worked as a policy analyst in the Executive and Legislative branches of City of Porto Alegre, Brazil, where I got to work on important economic reforms. I discuss policy issues as a regular contributor to Exame, and my policy papers have been featured in some of the most popular media outlets in Brazil.
I also run the Public Choice in Latin America project, with Nicolás Cachanosky.

title: Projects and Datasets
